title: Azure Service Bus queues pubsub component as stable
body:
## Describe the feature
The Azure Service Bus Queues pubsub component requires certification tests to be moved from Beta to Stable.
https://docs.dapr.io/reference/components-reference/supported-pubsub/setup-azure-servicebus-queues/

## Release Note
<!-- How should this new feature be announced in our release notes? It can be populated later. -->
<!-- Keep it as a single line. Examples: -->

<!-- RELEASE NOTE: **ADD** New feature in Dapr. -->
<!-- RELEASE NOTE: **FIX** Bug in runtime. -->
<!-- RELEASE NOTE: **UPDATE** Runtime dependency. -->

RELEASE NOTE: ADD Azure Service Bus Queues pubsub component as stable.

comments.body: Hi, could you please clarify the current status of Azure Service Bus Queue in Dapr pub/sub? Is it considered preview, stable, or GA? Also wondering if there’s a roadmap or known limitations for production use. Thanks!

Hey @Sarah-Aly  its still officially in beta support as per docs [here](https://docs.dapr.io/reference/components-reference/supported-pubsub/). It is used in production fairly heavily however, but the test coverage isn't as completed as say Azure service bus topics.

I will be working over the coming weeks on adding the certification tests required to meet the Stable criteria, focusing on:

- Pub/sub conformance scenarios aligned with Dapr certification guidelines.
- Behavior and reliability under failure conditions (retries, lock expiration, message redelivery).
- Coverage parity with the existing tests for Azure Service Bus Topics.

The goal is to provide sufficient automated evidence so the component can be promoted from Beta to Stable with confidence.

Regards
Hello @alicejgibbons & @Sarah-Aly  I have already created the PRs to certify the component. Regards.
Hi, are there expectation date when it will go to stable?
